I don’t see the tension. Early feedback may give negative results if you rush and show people unfinished product. However this is hard to achieve in a corporation, since you often pushed to move faster. As a creator you should have a couple of years alone with your creation.

To elaborate on the two sides: there's a time when a project needs a couple of years alone with its creator (as <@UMYKGHYBA> said), and that works best in a large company managing a portfolio of products (like Xerox). But there comes a time when a project needs execution, and that works best in a company that's all-in on it, providing a sense of urgency and consequence.

So it's not so much that people are good at one or the other, it seems to me. It's the company culture that sets the environment to encourage one phase or another.

my guess is bugs related to trying to support iOS 13 dark mode. I few months ago I made some sites support dark mode. it turned out to be a ton of work. At first it was just a few css lines to the sites' style sheet but then it turned out there were hand styled tables (not in the style sheet), static images and diagrams the looked bad on dark backgrounds, and live diagrams drawn with code that had to be refactored to check the mode dark/light and use a different set of colors to draw themselves. I still run across the occasion thing I missed even 5 months later.

formal proofs can't even prove tic tac toe is correct. They have not advanced to include the concept of something being drawn, or event-driven situations. But any purely computational type of task, like calculating the value of cosine(n), they can guarantee all sorts of good things. So they have made steady progress, but academia doesn't seem to want to move out of the protected grove of pure numerical types of programs.  I am not sure if they find it too grubby and boring, or it is just too hard to do. But what product today doesn't draw something or send something across the network? So at the moment languages like Coq and Agda and the other experimental logic languages are so far removed from practicality, that when you talk to these people as i occasionally do, it's like talking to someone from another planet.

It’s fashionable at the moment to criticize capitalism. It’s not ideal but what alternative is there that hasn’t killed millions of people and been far worse?
It’s fashionable at the moment to criticize capitalism. It’s not ideal but what alternative is there that hasn’t killed millions of people and been far worse?

The alternative is yet to be explrored. I guess Its the old tale of reformation vs. revolution. Can "capitalism" be transformed into a sustainable way of living together? Or do we have to "create"  something new. Creating new words and concepts which people will associate with and which enable a sustainable way of living together.
